pr.title: Rename gm_17C0 to gmregclear based on assert
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed the game-mode module and header from the address-derived provisional name gm_17C0 to gmregclear. The diff is almost entirely a coordinated file/include/config rename: split metadata, configure.py object registration, exported gm headers, direct consumers, and one documentation comment were updated while the object remained NonMatching. The title says the new name is based on an assert, but the slice does not include the assert text or location.
what_changed: Renamed src/melee/gm/gm_17C0.c to src/melee/gm/gmregclear.c and changed its self-include from gm_17C0.h to gmregclear.h.
Renamed src/melee/gm/gm_17C0.h to src/melee/gm/gmregclear.h and updated the header guard from MELEE_GM_17C0_H to MELEE_gmregclear_H.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so the same text/rodata/data ranges are attributed to melee/gm/gmregclear.c instead of melee/gm/gm_17C0.c.
Updated configure.py so the object list now uses Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmregclear.c") instead of the old gm_17C0.c path.
Replaced old gm_17C0.h include paths in gm_1601.h, gm_unsplit.h, grcorneria.c, and vi0401.c with gmregclear.h.
Kept gmregclear.h exported through gm_unsplit.h with the IWYU pragma and updated a lbbgflash.c comment that listed gm_17C0.c as a game-mode caller.
smart_moves: The rename was propagated through both source includes and build/split metadata, avoiding the common decomp rename failure where the C file changes but the object map still references the old basename.
The PR preserved the object's NonMatching status and the split address ranges, making the change a low-risk provenance/name cleanup rather than a matching claim.
Updating gm_unsplit.h maintained the umbrella game-mode header export path for downstream files that rely on transitive gm includes.
Updating lbbgflash.c's comment removed a stale grep hit for the provisional gm_17C0 name, which helps future searches converge on the assert-derived module name.
decomp_lessons: Assert strings can justify replacing address-based module names with more meaningful original or near-original names, but future records should capture the exact assert text/location when available.
A file/module rename in this repository needs synchronized edits in configure.py, config/GALE01/splits.txt, the .c self-include, the .h guard, umbrella headers, and direct consumer includes.
Renames can improve source organization and symbol provenance even when the object remains NonMatching; do not conflate naming cleanup with codegen progress.
After renaming a provisional gm_<address> module, grep for the old basename in comments as well as include directives and build config.
When changing exported headers, preserve IWYU pragma annotations so include-cleanup tooling and transitive header expectations remain stable.
naming_conventions: gm_17C0 was treated as an address-derived provisional game-mode module name.
gmregclear is the new lowercase module basename, similar in style to nearby gmregcommon.c.
Public include paths changed from <melee/gm/gm_17C0.h> or "gm/gm_17C0.h" to gmregclear.h equivalents.
The header guard was updated to MELEE_gmregclear_H as shown in the diff; no review text in the slice explains the mixed-case guard style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly rewrite or function-level matching tactic is present in the diff.
The split entry kept the same section ranges, including .text 0x8017C0C8-0x8018325C and .data 0x803D7A50-0x803D9248, indicating a metadata-only rename.
configure.py continued to mark the object as NonMatching after the rename.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice.
The only evidence for the assert-derived name is the PR title; the exact assert was not included in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Search the current tree for gm_17C0 to verify no stale references remain.
Find the assert string or source line that identifies the module as gmregclear and document it beside the rename.
Audit gmregclear.h header guard casing against current project conventions.
Look for other gm_<address> modules near gmregcommon/gmregclear that have assert-derived names available.
Use the gmregclear name to guide future function and struct naming inside the renamed NonMatching object.
